summary: Stancil argues that dashboards and reporting aren’t distractions from “real” analytics work; they are how companies construct a shared reality in an abstract, non-physical world. Metrics don’t just measure the business—they define it—so consistent dashboards are foundational for trust and decision-making.
money_quotes: Data and metrics—revenues, retention rates, product usage patterns—don’t measure a company’s world; they are its world.
The code is the concept, and the concept is the code.
Every dashboard is a window into a Pixar scene, and every metric is a rendering of it.
Looking at two dashboards that don’t match is like looking out two adjacent windows and not seeing the same thing.
key_insight: Dashboards are essential because they create the consistent, shared reality that companies need to operate in an abstract world.
